title: Full-Stack Developer (Python/FastAPI + React) - Compliance SaaS with Large-File Parsing

client.signals: Domain-mature: spec written, architecture decided, company incorporated, design prototype ready. Seeking reliable mid-to-senior build partner, not exploratory discovery. Structured procurement (6 milestones, written DoD, payment gates). EU preference suggests possible European team or timezone discipline. Additional modules planned post-launch indicates confidence in product roadmap.

summary: Client is building a B2B SaaS platform that automates compliance workflows by ingesting large XML/PDF files (500+ MB), applying 170+ reconciliation rules, and delivering analytical dashboards. They have a complete technical spec, field-level parsing reference, and design prototype ready. They need a reliable full-stack developer to implement the product alongside the Product Owner over 6 milestones with strict testing, memory profiling, and multi-tenant isolation.

explicit_requirements.text: Unified file-format detector that identifies document type, period and version from uploaded XML and PDF files and routes to correct parser
Streaming XML parsers using lxml/iterparse, memory-stable on files up to 500+ MB; PDF parsing with embedded XML extraction
Asynchronous processing with Celery + Redis featuring separate heavy/light queues, status polling, full job lifecycle
Business rules engine with 170+ documented reconciliation rules across multiple document types, configurable thresholds, drill-down to source transactions
Analytical dashboard with tables, charts, trend and anomaly analysis, server-side pagination for 100,000+ row datasets, Excel and PDF export
Multi-tenant architecture with full data isolation per client account, enforced and tested at API level
Subscription billing integration with Stripe (trial, plans, proration, webhooks, cancellation)
Backend implemented in Python with FastAPI
Frontend implemented in React + TypeScript
Database: PostgreSQL
Queue: Celery + Redis
Storage: AWS S3 or Azure Blob
Auth behind adapter module: Supabase or Auth0
Payments: Stripe
Hosting: AWS or Azure
Each milestone includes automated tests: golden-file test suites against pre-validated fixtures, minimum 70% coverage on business logic, static analysis report with zero critical findings
Milestone 2 includes a payment gate: live proof of concept parsing 500+ MB XML file via iterparse with memory profile, before rest of milestone proceeds
Weekly written status update delivered on Mondays
Demo call at end of each milestone following pre-agreed script (agreed 3 days in advance)
Fixed-price, milestone-based contract; total budget approx. EUR 4,000 across 6 milestones
APPLICATION REQUIRED: What is the largest XML or structured file you have processed in production, and what was your approach to memory management?
APPLICATION REQUIRED: How would you enforce tenant isolation in a FastAPI + PostgreSQL application, and at which layers?

implicit_needs: Demonstrable large-file processing experience (XML or CSV) with memory management discipline
Deep familiarity with multi-tenant isolation patterns and async job architecture
Clean code and test discipline (70%+ coverage, zero critical static analysis findings)
Honest, reliable communication and consistent delivery (weekly updates, predictable milestone closure)
Willingness to do low-risk paid probation task (parse real XML via REST endpoint) before full commitment
Domain knowledge not required but ability to work from detailed spec and field-level reference documentation
Understanding of compliance/accounting domain nuances will accelerate delivery
domain: Fiscal and accounting compliance SaaS; business process automation; large-file ETL; reconciliation rule engines; analytical dashboarding
success_signals: Candidate demonstrates shipped SaaS end-to-end (backend, frontend, ops)
Candidate has concrete production experience parsing files 50+ MB with proven memory-safe approach (iterparse, chunking, streaming)
Candidate articulates multi-tenant isolation strategy (row-level security, tenant_id filters, schema isolation, or combination)
Candidate shows discipline: test/coverage rigor, honest timelines, clear communication of blockers
Candidate willing to do low-risk probation task (parse real XML, return REST response) to validate fit before 6-month commitment
Candidate comfortable with fixed-price, milestone-based risk (not T&M)
Candidate familiar with or willing to learn configured rules-engine pattern (170+ rules, thresholds, drill-down)
timeline: 6 milestones with weekly status updates (Mondays) and demo calls at milestone close; no overall timeline given but structured milestone cadence implies 3-4 months likely
red_flags: Fixed-price EUR 4,000 is low for the stated scope (file parsing, rules engine 170+ rules, dashboard, multi-tenant, Stripe billing, async queue, testing discipline). High fixed-price risk if scope drifts or hidden edge cases emerge.
Milestone 2 payment gate (500+ MB iterparse PoC with memory profile) acts as a probation gate; candidate must deliver this on spec before proceeding. If memory profile fails, milestone 2 halts.
Compliance domain implies real business liability; wrong reconciliation rules or data isolation bugs have audit/regulatory consequences. Testing rigor is non-optional.

Client mentions 'well-argued alternatives can be discussed at kickoff; stack will not change mid-project' -- suggests limited flexibility once kickoff spec is locked. Contractor must commit to the decided stack.
No mention of existing codebase or partial build; implies build from scratch. Scope is large for 6 months if discovery/spec clarification is needed.
EU timezone preference and weekly Monday status requirement imply operational overhead and timezone alignment cost.

demo_concepts.name: Large-File Streaming XML Parser Demo
demo_concepts.what: In-browser React app that accepts a mock 500+ MB XML file (or 50+ MB truncated), parses it via iterparse logic (simulated in JS or compiled to WASM), displays memory usage chart in real time, and shows extracted structured records in a table.
demo_concepts.hero_feature: Memory-efficient streaming parse with live memory profiling visualization, proving the core R2 capability (lxml/iterparse stability on huge files) in a reproducible, auditable way.
demo_concepts.why_wins: Directly proves R2 (streaming XML on 500+ MB files with memory discipline). Client's Milestone 2 payment gate is exactly a 500+ MB iterparse PoC with memory profile; this demo pre-answers that question. Candidate can use this as a starting template for the real implementation. Shows honest understanding of the constraint and memory management approach.

demo_concepts.name: Multi-Tenant Isolation Dashboard
demo_concepts.what: React + TypeScript app showing a mock multi-tenant SaaS dashboard where two tenant accounts are fully isolated. Clicking between tenants shows different data sets, different Stripe subscription states, and enforced row-level access. Code comments explain isolation layers (row_tenant_id filters, RBAC, data scope enforcement).
demo_concepts.hero_feature: Tenant switching with enforced data isolation at query level, demonstrating understanding of R6 (multi-tenant isolation enforced at API level).
demo_concepts.why_wins: Directly addresses Q2 (tenant isolation in FastAPI + PostgreSQL). Candidate articulates layers (API middleware, row filters, schema scoping, RLS). Shows product understanding and audit-ready architecture thinking. Safe to build as frontend-only mockup with hardcoded tenant IDs.

demo_concepts.name: Reconciliation Rules Engine & Anomaly Dashboard
demo_concepts.what: Interactive dashboard (React + TypeScript) showing a mock reconciliation run: input data fed into a rules engine simulator, 170+ rules applied (summarized as rule categories), anomalies highlighted, drill-down to source transactions, and export buttons (CSV, PDF mockups).
demo_concepts.hero_feature: Rules engine decision flow with anomaly highlighting and drill-down capability, demonstrating R4 (business rules engine with 170+ rules, drill-down to source) and R5 (analytical dashboard with anomaly detection).
demo_concepts.why_wins: Proves candidate understands the compliance domain and the rules-engine + analytics flow. Candidate can show how rules are structured, how anomalies surface, and how drill-down works. All doable with mock data and no backend. Gives client confidence in domain comprehension before committing EUR 4,000.
